brief.name: LUMINA
brief.industry: Electric concept-car launch experience for a premium automotive brand
brief.audience: Design-conscious premium buyers and EV enthusiasts aged 28-50. Many arrive on mobile from a social teaser, then revisit on desktop. They expect cinematic storytelling but still need real specs (range, charge time, 0-100) before reserving a test drive.
brief.tone: cinematic, premium, confident, restrained, future-facing, quietly technical
brief.references: Apple AirPods/Vision Pro product pages, Polestar.com, Zaha Hadid material studies, Blade Runner 2049 color grading, Igarashi Design motion reels
brief.context: Full-bleed hero must reveal the vehicle silhouette with one bold headline and a single 'Reserve a test drive' CTA. Each scroll scene is a distinct chapter: exterior design, interior experience, powertrain, sustainability. Interactive moments expose 3-5 vehicle hotspots (lighting, wheels, yoke, battery). Specs (range 580km, 18-min 10-80% charge, 0-100 in 3.4s) must appear as a clear factual panel before the final reservation CTA. Respect prefers-reduced-motion: the whole story must still read as a clean vertical stack with no scroll choreography.
brief.avoid: Falling back to a generic SaaS landing pattern. Motion in every section. Burying the specs behind interaction only. Auto-playing audio. Stock photos of people. Marketing words like 'revolutionary' or 'game-changing'. Carousels that trap keyboard focus.
